conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who's 4 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with Alex and are you the main person looking after them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex lives with me, but their other parent also lives with us and they're the one who already claims Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. That's an important detail. 

Can I just confirm - you, Alex, and Alex's other parent all live together in the same household, and the other parent is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. We all live together and Alex's other parent is the one claiming the Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: Under UK Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child. Since Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you cannot also make a claim for the same child. It doesn't matter that you live together and are both responsible for Alex - only one person per child can receive the benefit.

If circumstances change in the future (for example, if the other parent stops claiming or if living arrangements change), the eligibility situation could be different. But as things stand currently, with Alex's other parent already claiming, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 4 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority; Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: According to UK Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. In this case, Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Since both parents live together in the same household with Alex, and the other parent is the existing claimant, the person enquiring cannot also claim Child Benefit for the same child. Only one person can get Child Benefit for each child, so a second claim for Alex would not be eligible.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives with their 4-year-old child Alex and Alex's other parent in the same household. The other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Under UK Child Benefit rules, only one person can receive Child Benefit for each child. Since Alex's other parent is already the claimant, this person is not eligible to also claim Child Benefit for Alex.
